name: frontmatter-has-name-and-description
assertion: SKILL.md YAML frontmatter contains only 'name' and 'description' fields

name: description-defers-non-rbac-drift
assertion: description explicitly says NOT to use this skill for registry/spec drift unrelated to authorization, pointing to ssss-registry-parity-auditor instead

name: probe-clean-on-fixed-engine
assertion: node scripts/probe-rbac.mjs, run against this repo's current src/engine.mjs, reports 0 fail-open findings and 0 unexpectedly-denied cases

name: probe-catches-the-historical-regression
assertion: reintroducing the original `envelope.actor?.role || 'admin'` fail-open default into a scratch copy of src/engine.mjs and running probe-rbac.mjs against that copy produces exactly 1 fail-open finding for the missing-actor-denied scenario, and 0 for every other scenario

name: does-not-false-flag-intentional-bypasses
assertion: the system-role-bypass-intentional and admin-role-hardcoded-intentional scenarios are never reported as fail-open findings, since both are documented, deliberate design choices

name: emit-negative-fixtures-produces-valid-shape
assertion: node scripts/emit-negative-fixtures.mjs, given a findings file with at least one fail-open entry, emits JSON objects with id/name/description/request/expected_response/expected_http_status fields matching conformance/fixtures.json's existing fixture shape

name: fresh-idempotency-keys-per-probe
assertion: every envelope generated by enumerate-envelopes.mjs has a freshly-generated idempotency_key (crypto.randomUUID()), never a hardcoded or reused value, since a reused key would return a cached response instead of re-running Stage 5.5
